01
BUILD-UP BREAKOUT
// TIGHT CONSOLIDATION → EXPLOSION — YOUR #1 SETUP //
// BUILD-UP BREAKOUT REFERENCE //
// WHAT IT IS
Tight consolidation where price compresses near resistance — sellers can't push it down, buyers absorb every dip
Energy coiling — tighter range = more explosive breakout
LTPro APEX "BRK IMMINENT" = build-up at critical level
// ENTRY RULES
Option 1: Green candle closes above resistance → enter
Option 2 (YOUR WAY): Wait for resistance-to-support flip after retest
FAKEOUT: SIGNAL stays WAIT/HOLD after break → DO NOT ENTER
Stop loss below the build-up range
// ENTRY CHECKLIST
BIAS flip on 5-min chart
VWAP aligned with direction
SIGNAL = BUY (not WAIT or HOLD)
AT LEVEL or NEAR ZONE confirmed
Candlestick confirmation on 1-min
Breakout candle closed above level
// DO NOT ENTER IF
SIGNAL = WAIT — full stop
Price in MID-RANGE
First candle after break, no retest yet
High-impact news within 30 min
QQQ and NVDA diverging from SPY
VIX spiking — wait for resolution
02
SMART MONEY CONCEPTS
// 9 CORE CONCEPTS — WHERE THE BIG MONEY MOVES //
// 9 SMC CONCEPTS //
01
Order Blocks
Last candle before impulse. Bullish OB = demand. Bearish OB = supply.
02
Fair Value Gaps
3-candle imbalance. Price fills FVGs — they are magnets.
03
Supply & Demand
Zones not lines. Resistance becomes support on flip.
04
Change of Character
CHoCH = early trend shift warning.
05
Break of Structure
BOS = trend continuation confirmed.
06
Liquidity Pools
Stop clusters above highs / below lows. Smart money hunts these.
07
Stop-Loss Hunting
Fakeout spike grabs stops then reverses. LTPro SWEEP row detects this.
08
False Breakouts
SIGNAL stays HOLD = fakeout confirmed.
09
Kill Zones
NY open 9:30-11 AM ET = peak institutional activity.
03
CHART PATTERNS
// RANGE · BREAKOUT · REVERSAL — KNOW YOUR CATEGORY //
// RANGE / BREAKOUT / REVERSAL //
RANGE — WAIT FOR BREAK
DESCENDING CHANNELASCENDING CHANNELRECTANGLEDIVERGING △
BREAKOUT — YOUR PRIMARY SETUPS
SYMMETRICAL △ASCENDING △DESCENDING △FLAG
REVERSAL — EXIT OR FADE
HEAD & SHOULDERSINV. H&SDOUBLE TOPDOUBLE BOTTOM
// LTP CONNECTION
Breakout = LTPro triangle detection. APEX fires at apex compression.
Range = MID-RANGE dashboard. No entry until break + SIGNAL flip.
Reversal at S/R = watch for SWEEP → CHoCH → new direction.
Identify the category before the open.
04
CANDLE PATTERNS
// FINAL GATE BEFORE TRIGGER //
// BEARISH & BULLISH CANDLE REFERENCE //
BULLISH
Hammer / Inv. Hammer
Long lower wick at support. Rejection of lower prices.
Bullish Engulfing ★★★
75-80% accuracy. Large green engulfs red.
Morning Star ★★★
70-75% accuracy. Bear → Doji → Bull.
Three White Soldiers ★★★
80%+ accuracy. Three consecutive bull candles.
Abandoned Baby ★★
70% accuracy. Gap + doji + gap back.
BEARISH
Shooting Star / Hanging Man
Long upper wick at resistance. Classic put signal.
Bearish Engulfing ★★★
Large red engulfs green. Best at resistance.
Evening Star ★★★
72% accuracy. Bull → Doji → Bear.
Three Black Crows ★★★
78% accuracy. Three consecutive bear candles.
Bearish 3-Line Strike
84% → BULLISH reversal. After 3 reds, big green = buy.
// CANDLE WORKFLOW
BIAS → VWAP → SIGNAL = BUY → AT LEVEL → candle confirms → ENTER. Strong engulfing or hammer = take the shot. Doji = wait one more.
05
4 TYPES OF GAPS
// CLASSIFY PRE-MARKET — SETS YOUR MORNING BIAS //
// GAP CLASSIFICATION REFERENCE //
1. Common Gap
Appears in trading range. Low interest. Usually fills quickly.
ACTION: Expect fill. Wait or fade.
2. Breakaway Gap
Gaps through key S/R level. Like a breakout via gap.
ACTION: Trade continuation.
3. Runaway Gap
Mid-trend gap. Strong trend signal.
ACTION: Enter on first VWAP pullback.
4. Exhaustion Gap
End of trend at important S/R. Last gasp of momentum.
ACTION: Fade after reversal candle.
// PRE-MARKET ROUTINE
Breakaway = trade with it. Exhaustion = fade after confirmation. Common = wait for fill.
06
VOLUME ANALYSIS
// CONFIRM THE MOVE — VOLUME TELLS YOU IF IT'S REAL //
// VOLUME CASES //
4 PRICE vs VOLUME SCENARIOS
PRICE ↑ VOL ↑
Confirmed uptrend. CALLS.
PRICE ↑ VOL ↓
Weak. Exit on reversal.
PRICE ↓ VOL ↑
Confirmed downtrend. PUTS.
PRICE ↓ VOL ↓
Weak. Exit on reversal.
// VOLUME STRENGTH CASES
Case 1 — Spike only: One spike then drops = weak trend. Don't chase.
Case 2 — Average: Consistent moderate = proceed with caution.
Case 3 — High Sustained: Volume building with price = confirms APEX breakout.
07
SUPPLY & DEMAND ZONES
// RALLY-BASE-DROP · RETEST = YOUR ENTRY //
// DEMAND — RBR & DBR //
// SUPPLY — RBD & DBD //
// DEMAND — CALL SETUPS
RBR: Rally → Base → Rally. Retest of base = call entry.
DBR: Drop → Base → Rally. Retest of base = call entry.
BASE = where institutions placed buy orders.
LTPro AT ZONE = price touching base. Wait for SIGNAL + candle.
// SUPPLY — PUT SETUPS
RBD: Rally → Base → Drop. Retest of base = put entry.
DBD: Drop → Base → Drop. Retest = put entry.
BASE = where institutions placed sell orders.
Rejection candle at base + SIGNAL flip = highest conviction put.
08
ORDER BLOCK RETEST
// 3 SCENARIOS — HOW PRICE RETESTS A SUPPLY ZONE //
// ORDER BLOCK RETEST SCENARIOS //
// 3 SCENARIOS
Significant High Fakeout: Spike above supply grabs stops then reverses. LTPro SWEEP row = this. Best put entry.
Consolidation Fakeout: Pops briefly above supply then fails. SIGNAL stays HOLD = confirmed fakeout.
Continuation: Breaks cleanly through supply. Zone flips to demand. Trade continuation UP.
// KEY INSIGHT
Same supply zone, 3 different outcomes depending on HOW price approaches. SIGNAL alignment is your confirmation.
09
ASCENDING TRIANGLE
// YOUR PRIMARY BREAKOUT PATTERN //
// ASCENDING TRIANGLE GUIDE //
// ENTRY OPTIONS
Option 1: Green candle closes above flat resistance.
Option 2 — YOUR METHOD: Wait for resistance-to-support flip after retest. Price pulls back, holds, confirms → ENTER.
Stop: Just below breakout level.
Target: Triangle height projected above breakout.
// LTP CONNECTION
APEX "BRK IMMINENT" = triangle approaching apex.
TEAL = RADAR (forming). RED/GREEN = SNIPER (direction confirmed).
Breakout Intelligence Layer = your take profit target.

11
RULES OF ENGAGEMENT
// NON-NEGOTIABLE — READ EVERY MORNING //
// ENTRY HIERARCHY
Step 1: BIAS flip on 5-min
Step 2: VWAP aligned
Step 3: SIGNAL = BUY
Step 4: AT LEVEL or NEAR ZONE
Step 5: Candlestick confirms 1-min
ALL 5 → EXECUTE
// HARD RULES
SIGNAL = WAIT is the primary gate. Nothing overrides this.
5-min = decision TF. 1-min = entry timing only.
After APEX — wait for candle OR retest. Never raw APEX entry.
MID-RANGE = no trade.
Choppy = sit out. Protect capital.
Max daily loss defined before open. Hit it = done.
// LEADING INDICATORS
QQQ: Aligned with SPY? Divergence = warning.
NVDA: Strong = tech breadth support for calls.
VIX: Spiking = wait. Falling = green light.
ES1!: Weekly + 1H direction before open.
// MINDSET
Patience is the edge. Setup comes to you — never chase.
No trades on a choppy day = a winning day.
Real money only validates the system.
Scale size gradually as confidence builds.

15
VIX ANALYSIS GUIDE
// FEAR GAUGE — READ VIX BEFORE EVERY TRADE //
// THE #1 VIX RULE — MEMORIZE THIS
VIX FALLING ↓
Fear leaving market
✅ GOOD FOR CALLS
❌ BAD FOR PUTS
VIX RISING ↑
Fear entering market
❌ BAD FOR CALLS
✅ GOOD FOR PUTS
// VIX LEVELS CHEAT SHEET
BELOW 15
COMPLACENCY — EASY MODE
Options cheap · Bull market calm · Best for calls · Full size ✅
15 — 20
NORMAL MARKET
Standard conditions · Trade normally · Normal size ✅
20 — 25
ELEVATED FEAR ← YOU ARE HERE
Market nervous · Bigger swings · Reduce size · Quick scalps only ⚠️
25 — 30
HIGH FEAR
Volatile · Fakeouts common · Half size max · Wider stops needed ⚠️
30 — 40
PANIC MODE
Very dangerous · Tiny size only · Consider sitting out 🚨
ABOVE 40
EXTREME PANIC — SIT OUT
2020 COVID = 85 · 2008 crisis = 80 · Once in years · Do not trade 🚨
// 4 VIX STATES
STATE 1 — VIX FALLING ↓
Spring loading for SPY rally · Watch closely · Wait for SIGNAL BUY on SPY
STATE 2 — VIX FLAT →
No edge from VIX · Use other indicators · Chop likely · Be selective
STATE 3 — VIX RISING SLOWLY ↑
Fear building · SPY calls risky · Consider puts · Reduce call size
STATE 4 — VIX SPIKING FAST ↑↑
EXIT ALL POSITIONS · Something happened · News event · Wait for calm · DO NOT trade into spike 🚨
// THREE TIMEFRAME VIX RULE
DAILY
STRATEGY LEVEL
"Should I trade today?" · Overall fear trend · Check every morning · Sets weekly tone
1 HOUR
TACTICAL LEVEL
"Is fear rising or falling this session?" · Check before entry · Intraday context
5 MIN
EXECUTION LEVEL
"Something happening RIGHT NOW?" · Monitor during trade · Spike = exit immediately
// SCORING SYSTEM
3/3 VIX bearish = STRONG green light for calls ✅✅✅ Full size
2/3 VIX bearish = Cautious green light ✅✅⚠️ Half size
1/3 VIX bearish = Red light for calls ⚠️⚠️ Consider puts
0/3 VIX bearish = Strong puts signal ⚠️⚠️⚠️ Do NOT buy calls
// VIX vs SPY RELATIONSHIP
SPY UP + VIX DOWN = IDEAL ✅ Confirmed bull move · Full size calls
SPY UP + VIX FLAT = OK ✅ Moderate confidence · Normal size calls
SPY UP + VIX UP = WARNING ⚠️ Rally not trusted · Half size or skip
SPY DOWN + VIX DOWN = WARNING ⚠️ Unusual · Could mean exhaustion · Watch carefully
SPY DOWN + VIX UP = NORMAL BEAR ✅ Confirmed down · Consider puts
VIX SPIKING = EXIT ALL 🚨 Something happened · Ask questions later
// VIX EFFECT ON OPTIONS PRICING
VIX LOW (below 15): Options are CHEAP · Better R:R · Best time to buy calls or puts
VIX NORMAL (15-20): Standard pricing · Normal R:R · Trade as usual
VIX HIGH (above 25): Options are EXPENSIVE · Worse R:R · You pay more for same strike
REAL EXAMPLE: SPY call at same strike costs $2.00 at VIX 15 vs $3.50 at VIX 25 = 75% more expensive!
// TODAY'S VIX CONTEXT
VIX at 25.08 = elevated · Options are expensive · Take profits faster · Use smaller size · Quick scalps only · Watch VIX direction at 9:30 open
// MAVERICK'S DAY OF WEEK THEORY + VIX
MONDAY: Historically bullish · If VIX falling = high conviction calls ✅
TUESDAY: Strongest day statistically · VIX falling + Tuesday = best call setup of week ✅
WEDNESDAY: Exhaustion/reversal risk · Watch VIX closely · FOMC risk · Reduce size ⚠️
THURSDAY: Watch and transition · Follow Wednesday's lead · Read VIX trend
FRIDAY: Complex · OpEx volatility · VIX often spikes EOD · Take profits early
// VIX MORNING ROUTINE — EVERY DAY
Step 1 — VIX Daily: Above or below 20? Rising or falling trend this week?
Step 2 — VIX 1H: Above or below VWAP? Rising or falling this session?
Step 3 — Score it: How many of 3 timeframes are bearish VIX? (bearish VIX = good for calls)
Step 4 — Size it: 3/3 = full size · 2/3 = half size · 1/3 = quarter size · 0/3 = puts or sit out
Step 5 — Monitor 5M: During trade watch VIX 5M · Spike = EXIT immediately no questions asked
// THE ONE SENTENCE RULE
VIX falling = calls have wings · VIX rising = puts have power · VIX spiking = get out now
